title: dianthus ciliatus subsp. ciliatus
intro: Dianthus ciliatus subsp. ciliatus, also known as the fringed pink or pink fringed pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family, characterized by its fringed petals and pink to red flowers. Traditionally used in folk medicine, this plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dianthus ciliatus subsp. ciliatus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
